operating_state: Total revenue reached $39.1 billion in Q2 2024, up 22% year-over-year, driven by advertising revenue growth with ad impressions and average price per ad each increasing 10%. Reality Labs contributed growth from consumer hardware sales despite deepened operating losses. Growth was broadly positive across all geographic regions with particular strength in Asia-Pacific, driven by online commerce, gaming, and entertainment verticals despite ongoing iOS targeting limitations.
cost_structure: Total costs and expenses increased to $24.2 billion from $22.6 billion year-over-year, with cost of revenue rising primarily from higher infrastructure and data center depreciation costs. Research and development expenses grew driven by 9% headcount growth in engineering functions and AI infrastructure investments. Reality Labs operating losses deepened as metaverse technology investments accelerated, while Family of Apps operating margin expanded to 50% from 41%.
material_events: The company initiated its first quarterly dividend program, paying $0.50 per share and completed $6.3 billion in share repurchases with $60.1 billion remaining authorized. A settlement was reached with Texas regarding facial recognition technology violations in July 2024. Multiple executive trading plans were established during the quarter, and the annual shareholder meeting approved amendments to officer liability limitations while rejecting dual class structure elimination proposals.
macro_exposures: The company faces escalated regulatory uncertainty from potential EU-U.S. Data Privacy Framework invalidation by CJEU, which could prevent Facebook and Instagram operations in Europe. European Commission opened formal DMA proceedings in March 2024 with preliminary adverse findings regarding subscription advertising models and data combination practices. Foreign currency headwinds reduced revenue while ongoing legal-related accruals increased from regulatory and litigation pressures.
forward_outlook: Management expects Reality Labs operating losses to increase meaningfully in 2024 as metaverse investments accelerate, with capital expenditures guidance of $37-40 billion for 2024 and significant growth expected in 2025 for AI infrastructure. The company maintains substantial non-cancelable contractual commitments primarily for servers and network infrastructure. Continued quarterly dividend payments are planned subject to board approval.
uncertainty_profile: FTC administrative proceedings seek substantial consent order modifications including restrictions on minor data usage and board composition changes, while multiple European investigations under Digital Services Act and Digital Markets Act create exposure to operational restrictions. The company faces potential federal tax liability from ongoing IRS transfer pricing disputes with Tax Court decision expected in 2024. Multiple class action lawsuits regarding youth mental health impacts and platform practices continue with unpredictable monetary and operational impacts.
